message:
fix(semantic_anomaly): reject non-finite embeddings at observe() entry (ft-c17iw)

Both SemanticAnomalyDetector::observe and ConformalAnomalyDetector::observe
normalize the input embedding via normalize_simd, then feed the normalized
vector into the EWMA centroid update. normalize_simd falls through to
passthrough when mag_sq is NaN (NaN > 0.0 is false), and 0.0 * Inf = NaN,
so a single non-finite component silently propagates:

  embedding[i] = NaN  →  normalized_emb[i] = NaN
    →  centroid[i] = (1-α) * centroid[i] + α * NaN  =  NaN
    →  every future similarity = NaN
    →  every future distance = (1.0 - NaN).max(0.0) = 0.0
    →  anomaly detection silently disabled forever

The EWMA fixpoint is a NaN-absorbing sink: once any centroid component
is NaN, renormalization preserves it (because normalize_simd's NaN
guard leaves the vector untouched) and detection never recovers.

Fix: reject embeddings with any non-finite component at observe() entry,
before any state mutation. Matches the fail-closed pattern of
ft-b4l62 (Histogram), ft-yskcu (continuous_backpressure), ft-0p5q5
(EValueMonitor::observe), ft-761tz (disk_pressure), ft-jz40p (time_series).

6 regression tests cover: NaN in first observe (must not initialize),
NaN after initialization (must not mutate centroid), +Inf and -Inf
(must also be rejected), and the same three scenarios for the Conformal
variant.

75 semantic_anomaly::tests pass.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.7 (1M context) <noreply@anthropic.com>
